D4 Colorimetry L3 Apply Reference

The NCS Triangle

slide the hue and read the natural-colour triangle where blackness, whiteness, and chromaticness sum to 100

NCS rests on the six elementary percepts — white, black and the four unique hues yellow, red, blue, green. Every colour is a blackness, whiteness, and chromaticness that add to 100.

Slide the hue around the Y-R-B-G circle. The triangle holds that hue's nuances: pure white at top, full chromatic colour at the right point, black at the bottom — every interior point a defined NCS notation.
